comment: FALSE-POSITIVE redaction fixtures. Each `value` MUST NOT be redacted. These are the exact false-positive classes spec §5.9 calls out (asset hashes, CSP nonces, SKUs, commit SHAs, cache-busted URLs). Synthetic strings only.

cases.label: asset-url-40-char-hash
cases.why: 40-char hex inside an asset URL — boundary rule says NOT a secret (no labeled context)

cases.label: csp-nonce-64-char
cases.why: 64-char hex CSP nonce. The 'nonce=' label is a deliberate spec carve-out — nonces appear constantly in HTML and are not secrets at rest.

cases.label: sku-64-char
cases.why: 64-char hex SKU embedded in narrative text — no labeled-context boundary

cases.label: commit-sha-40-char
cases.why: Git commit SHAs are 40-char hex but never represent a credential

cases.label: cache-busted-image-url
cases.why: Cache-busted image URL with 32-char hex query value. `v=` is a versioning param, not a secret label.

cases.label: version-string-with-hash
cases.why: Build hash inside a version string — no labeled-context boundary
